content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 1, 2010


Shawn Karl Mooney
P.O. Box 5193
San Mateo, CA 94402

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-10-041

Dear Mr. Mooney:

This letter addresses your request for written advice regarding the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").¹ After a careful review of the facts you have submitted, we are unable to provide
written advice to you. This letter is to notify you that we are withdrawing your advice request.
It is the Commission's policy not to answer purely hypothetical questions, or questions where the
facts presented are insufficient or too vague to render specific advice. (Regulation
18329(b)(2)(B), (8)(C) and (c)(4)(D), (F).) We note, your question also appear to involve
analysis of facts which have already occurred, and are the subject of a complaint you filed. We
decline to advise on this basis as well. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and (c)(4)(A).)

If we are incorrect, you can resubmit your advice request with your spouse's name, state
bar number, the name of her employer. We would also need to know which office you were
seeking and at which election you plan to declare. Under those circumstances, we could advise
you on your potential duties under the Act.

Since we do not have sufficient information to advise you with respect to your question,
we would suggest you refer to a document on the Commission's website for general information
on your questions:

• 2009/2010 Form 700, Statement of Economic Interests, Reference Pamphlet
(http://www.fppc.ca.gov/forms/700-09-10/RefPamphlet09-10.pdf)

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

W-10-041
Page No. 2


Should you have any additional questions on this matter, please contact the Commission
at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought advice regarding potential duties and filing requirements under the Political Reform Act, but the specific question details were not preserved in the withdrawal letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because the question was either hypothetical with insufficient facts or involved matters already subject to a complaint filed by the requestor. The FPPC indicated it would reconsider if the request were resubmitted with specific information including the spouse's name, state bar number, employer name, and relevant office/election details.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter with clear CONCLUSION section. No verbatim QUESTION section exists in the document as provided. The letter explains reasons for withdrawal under Regulations 18329(b)(2)(B), (8)(C), and (cX4)(D), (F), and 18329(bX8)(A) and (cXaXA). OCR quality is generally good with minor artifacts ('POLRRRCAL' appears to be 'POLITICAL').

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor sought advice regarding potential duties and filing requirements under the Political Reform Act, but the specific question details were not preserved in the withdrawal letter.

CONCLUSION: This letter is to notify you that we are withdrawing your advice request. It is the Commission's policy not to answer purely hypothetical questions, or questions where the facts presented are insufficient or too vague to render specific advice. We note, your question also appears to involve analysis of facts which have already occurred, and are the subject of a complaint you filed. We decline to advise on this basis as well.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew an advice request from Shawn Karl Mooney regarding the Political Reform Act, citing insufficient facts, hypothetical nature of the question, and the fact that the underlying matter was already the subject of a complaint. The FPPC suggested resubmission with additional specific information if the requestor wished to pursue written advice.
